  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Configuring Standard API in S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Generating OData of standard API :

First we have to login to SAP business accelerator hub account and go to SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public edition.

 

Choose a standard API as shown below

 

 

 In this case I have choose the product master standard API.

 

Select the overview tab, under that we can see Communication Scenario ID.

 

 

Copy communication scenario from standard API. Now Move to the

 S/4Hana cloud system -> Communication Arrangements

Select the Option New, and add a Scenario ID which we have copied from the Standard API

 

Create new communication system:

Search for Communication System in S/4 HANA cloud, here we need to create user and password for all OData services

Click on New, 

System will ask for new communication system id and system name. we can give the ID & Name of our choice. And Click on create. 

Give the Host name as shown below,  

In User for Inbound Communication, click on Add and Create New Inbound Communication User. Click on “New User” 

Give the username and description and select on proposed password to get the password which will give by system itself. (Copy the user name and password and paste somewhere so that, Once the user and password has created we can use the same for all the OData service Consumption.) 

Click on create 

Click on Save  

Then add your custom communication system in this communication arrangement

Then you get required ODATA URL

Now will connect S/4HANA Cloud to Power BI or any other reporting tools through OData Feed. Example:Open Power BI->get Data->OData feed.

In the OData feed enter the OData URL generated in S/4HANA Cloud. And give user name and password that we have generated in S4hana cloud “Communication System” to get the OData url.

Then Click on connect.

Select the checkbox to data preview and click on Transform data or Load.

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP S/4HANA Product Engineering essentials?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Applying SAP S/4HANA Product Engineering learning journey

Overview
In this learning journey, you’ll discover how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition can help you manage a product development with Product Engineering.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to:

  • model a product development process.
  • identify the business roles associated with Product Engineering.
  • maintain the master data relevant for this process.
  • perform system tasks related to those processes.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 

 

We are here to support you. I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Webinar Alert : Delivering Seamless S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas
entry.content:
Delivering Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transformation for Oil and Gas Companies

Join our KaarTech webinar series if you are looking for ways to ease the pressure to modernize your ERP solution landscape
 
Are you grappling with legacy ERP systems that hinder growth and efficiency? Is data chaos and operational inefficiency keeping you awake at night? You're not alone. Many organizations face these challenges daily. That's why KaarTech invites you to join our exclusive webinar series where we delve into the transformative journey of a major oil and gas company.
 
Addressing Pain Points :
  • Overwhelmed by Legacy Systems: Learn how to overcome the hurdles posed by outdated ERP systems that fragment operations.
  • Data Chaos: Discover strategies to tackle data silos and integrity issues, crucial for accurate decision-making amidst diverse data sources.
  • Resistance to Change: Navigate through team resistance with proven tactics to make your S/4HANA transition smoother.
Why Attend? This 3-part series goes beyond theory to provide actionable insights and strategies tailored for SAP customers worldwide. Each session equips you with practical insights for a risk-free S/4HANA transformation from ERP core analysis to Tech Debt mitigation and change management.
 
Key Sessions Include:
  • Part 1 - Analyzing Your Existing SAP Investments and Assessing Technical Debt
  • Part 2 - Data Migration Strategies in Preparation for Transformation
  • Part 3 - Managing Your Organization's Resistance To Change
By the series end, you'll have:
  • A clear roadmap to improve your ERP core.
  • Strategies to optimize edge systems for enhanced supply chain efficiency.
  • Best practices for data management crucial for S/4HANA readiness.
  • Actionable insights into effective change management.
Join us for an insightful journey as we decode the path to SAP S/4HANA transformation. This isn't just another webinar; it's your guide to overcoming challenges and seizing opportunities in the digital era.
 
REGISTER NOW

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Oil, Gas, and Energy Digital Technologies Digital Transformation

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas and Adapt UI for classic apps are growing in popularity. July 2024 update
entry.content:
It seems like generative AI is on everyone’s mind. This was certainly the case at SAP Sapphire last month. SAP continues to innovate in this area with new AI design patterns appearing across the product portfolio. In my opinion, one factor that’s driving the interest in AI for UX use cases is that people are looking for a fast and easy way to improve the user experience. The good news is that this does not require generative or even “classic” AI. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition includes a simple and powerful feature to adapt the UI for classic apps. This is also available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-prem customers as SAP Screen Personas.

In this post, I’ll provide a short background on SAP Screen Personas, discuss its implementation as a standard feature in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and share some interesting usage patterns we are observing. I’ll conclude with some concrete next steps so you can improve the user experience of your SAP ERP system.

SAP Screen Personas / Adapt UI for classic apps lets you simplify screens and streamline business processes

We introduced the first version of SAP Screen Personas in 2012. Fast forward to today and we are on SAP Screen Personas 3.0 SP18. During that time, thousands of customers have simplified their transaction screens. My colleagues and I, along with the customers that attend our monthly practitioner forum calls, have seen a wide variety of UX transformations, ranging from merging content from different tabs or transactions onto a single screen to automating bulk updates that reduce the process time from hours to 30 seconds.

SAP Screen Personas is built into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Screen Personas was so popular and loved by our customers that we decided to make it a standard feature of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Although there are over 3000 SAP Fiori apps available in SAP S/4HANA, there are still some scenarios that lack SAP Fiori coverage. Several thousand classic apps are available to accomplish these less-common tasks. There was already the ability to adapt SAP Fiori apps using SAPUI5 flexibility so we added the new functionality to adapt classic apps using the same menu.

Explosive growth in Adapt UI for classic apps in the past year

Running our software in the cloud gives us better visibility into how customers are using our solutions, which provides insights into adoption patterns. The team and I were pleasantly surprised to see that roughly 20% of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers are using the Adapt UI functionality. In fact, we have a 300% increase in the number of flavors running in productive systems within the past year. What are all these customers doing?

What we observed is that public cloud customers are simplifying many of the same apps that their on-prem counterparts do. These tend to be some of the more complex SAP GUI transactions in procurement, sales and distribution, and material handling. Looking deeper, we see that there is also the “long tail” of screen simplifications, with around 175 classic apps having 10 or fewer flavors.

From my perspective, the implications for SAP are clear. For the heavily used flavors, we should probably provide an SAP Fiori app. In some cases, this already exists, and we need to explore why customers are choosing to simplify the classic app rather than using the corresponding SAP Fiori app. (If this is you, please reach out; I would love to hear your explanation.)

Another conclusion I reached from this data is that it was a great idea to include Adapt UI for Classic Apps as a standard feature in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, since so many customers are using it to personalize screens to meet their unique business requirements. It also shows that public cloud customers have lots of flexibility to adapt or extend the user interface and they are using it. SAP Screen Personas is one of several powerful options. Private cloud or on-prem customers have even more extensibility options.

How to improve the user experience of your SAP ERP system

Customers

  • If you are an SAP S/4HANA customer, I recommend you use SAP Fiori apps, whenever possible. You can find the comprehensive list in the SAP Fiori app reference library.
  • For the classic apps, if there is something you don’t like, change it.
  • While SAP Screen Personas is built into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it’s a free add-on that takes less than an hour* to install into your private cloud or on-prem system. Then, you can simplify your SAP GUI transactions or classic apps, if you prefer that terminology.
    * if you know what you are doing.

Partners

Learn how Adapt UI for Classic Apps fits into SAP’s UX portfolio and how to use it strategically as part of an SAP S/4HANA transformation or greenfield implementation. The most comprehensive training course on SAP Screen Personas is now on the SAP Learning platform. This training is still free.

Learn more about SAP Screen Personas

  • Online training. The best way to learn the basics about SAP Screen Personas (Adapt UI for classic applications) is to take our course  on the SAP Learning platform. https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas
  • Connect with your peers. Every other month, we have a group of customers that gather online to share best practices, demo their flavors, and answer each other’s questions. Experts from the product team also join to explain how to use the latest functionality. If you are interested in joining, you can sign up online to join the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum. This will add you to the mailing list for the monthly meeting summaries. Please contact me directly if you want to join the monthly calls.

I hope you start using Adapt UI for Classic Apps or SAP Screen Personas to make your business users happier and more productive.

For the SAP UX Engineering team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: OpenSAP sunset/end of life - move to SAP Learning what it means.
entry.content:
OpenSAP sunset/end of life - move to SAP Learning what it means.

6 minute read.

 

As many of you may already know OpenSAP, open.sap.com will no longer be the go to portal for SAP learning as all new offerings will be given on learning.sap.com instead. Tuesday 30th July 2024 has now been confirmed as the end of life for OpenSAP. With some courses on the platform finishing as recently as June 2024 the opportunity for self study and a certificate of participation may still be of interest to many as course materials can still be downloaded, including slides, transcripts and videos. Don’t have time?, don't worry, over 100 courses have been migrated to SAP Learning.

 

What is OpenSAP? In OpenSAP’s own words:

 

About openSAP

openSAP is SAP’s free learning platform for everyone interested in learning about SAP’s latest innovations and how to survive in the digital economy. openSAP Enterprise MOOCs are complete courses, and learners can earn a certificate to demonstrate the knowledge they’ve acquired. Complementary learning opportunities exist in the form of openSAP Podcasts and openSAP Microlearning.

Source: open.sap.com

 

OpenSAP, the sister platform to OpenHPI, open.hpi.de of the Hasso Plattner Institute (which shall continue to offer its unique brand of Information and Communication Technology courses in both English and German) has been around since 2013, has accompanied many of us through a large part of our professional journeys. Therefore it’s with some nostalgia that we say goodbye to the tried and tested format of video lectures, quizzes and end of week assignments usually comprising of 30 questions in a multiple choice format similar to those of SAP Certification.  The twist with OpenSAP was that the weekly assignments and final exams were negatively marked so a wrong answer actually subtracted points from your score rather than simply receiving no marks.

 

With regards to SAP Certification the move to SAP Learning also signals a unification and democratization of SAP’s learning content and Certification programme.  Due to SAP’s pledge to upskill 2 million tech professionals by 2025 no longer is SAP Certification only the consequence of taking costly SAP Academies booked through SAP Training, training.sap.com or SAP Learning Hub, learninghub.sap.com. Now thousands of learning journeys can be accessed free of charge at beginner, intermediate and advanced level with certificates of completion provided also free of charge.  Where an SAP Certification is relevant or desired the user can choose to purchase a subscription to SAP Certification Hub (guidance is provided directly from the courses that lead to certification) and program an examination attempt once they feel ready to do so.

 

The following is a list of the courses I have had bookmarked for some time and I’m now getting round to consuming their valuable content to both broaden and deepen my understanding of SAP’s Analytics solutions and the complementary technologies that are being offered amongst other front-line subjects.  Some of the content has been updated for the new platform where applicable notably the quizzes at the end of each section but the vast majority of the content has been migrated as is to SAP Learning site.  So you don’t have to rush to download everything from OpenSAP unless your particular course of interest has not been migrated in which case there may already be a new version of the course on SAP Learning. If a new course has been created on SAP Learning or an existing course migrated this is mentioned in the course details of the courses on OpenSAP with a link to the new material as pictured below.

My top picks:

 

Introduction to SAP Datasphere (Repeat), 2023

SAP Analytic Cloud sister product for Data warehousing (formerly SAP Data Warehouse Cloud).

https://learning.sap.com/courses/introduction-to-sap-datasphere?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Building AI and Sustainability Solutions on SAP BTP, 2023

Week 5 is Collaborative Enterprise Planning in SAP Analytic Cloud applied to the course case study, Bagnoli & Co..  Otherwise a good deep dive into SAP’s sustainability strategy and Business Technology Platform (BTP) capabilities in Artificial Intelligence using SAP AI Core.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/building-ai-and-sustainability-solutions-on-sap-btp?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Using the Optimizer for SQLScript Programming in SAP HANA, 2023

If you’ve worked with HANA connections in SAC this is a great resource on performance tuning.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/using-the-optimizer-for-sqlscript-programming-in-sap-hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Two-Tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, 2018

One of the older offerings that caught my eye, unit 3 is on SAP Business Planning and Consolidation which is the precursor to SAC Planning so clients often ask that you are familiar with both solutions for content migration and hybrid environments.  The rest of the course is reinforcing your knowledge of SAP core processes on the cloud version of S/4 HANA.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/two-tier-erp-with-sap-s-4hana-cloud?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Gain Experience with SAP S/4HANA – Logistics (Repeat), 2022

Data Analysts, Engineers and Scientists can all benefit from deeper understanding of the business processes that underpin the data they work with. Covers Order to Cash, Plan to Product, EWM, TM and Procure to Pay.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/gain-experience-with-sap-s-4hana-logistics?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Insights into Selected Logistics Innovations in SAP S/4HANA, 2022

As with the previous offering this course provides a deeper understanding of advances in business processes help us bring more know how to data analytics.  Covers SRM, MRP, EAM and EWM.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/insights-into-selected-logistics-innovations-in-sap-s-4hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Gain Experience with a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA (Repeat), 2022

Part three of OpenSAP’s courses on System conversion, this is still a hot topic despite SAP moving its end of maintenance support for SAP ECC back to December 31st 2027.  Particularly clients on enhancement pack 5 or earlier will see their support run out at the end of 2025.  All SAP professionals should be versed in what this entails.  All three courses are available on SAP Learning Site, also search for:

“Key Technical Topics in a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA” and 

“Key Functional Topics in a System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA”

https://learning.sap.com/courses/gain-experience-with-a-system-conversion-to-sap-s-4hana?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course



Build Intelligent Data Apps with SAP HANA Cloud Multi-Model, 2023

Cloud is both the present and the future of many SAP services that are becoming must know knowledge for Application Developers, Consultants, Solution and Enterprise Architects.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/build-resilient-applications-on-sap-btp-with-amazon-web-services?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Build Resilient Applications on SAP BTP with Amazon Web Services, 2023

As the previous offering hyperscalers are both the present and the future of many SAP services therefore this is becoming a must know subject for Application Developers as well as Solution Architects, Consultants, Sales and Presales personnel.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/build-intelligent-data-apps-with-sap-hana-cloud-multi-model?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

What Can Blockchain Do for You?, 2022

A great breakdown of what Blockchain is and entails explained in largely none-technical terms to keep us all abreast of this new paradigm in the tech world.  This content has also been migrated to SAP Learning where any future updates to content can be added.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/what-can-blockchain-do-for-you?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Transform Your Business Processes with SAP Signavio and SAP Build, 2024

SAP Signavio is cutting edge technology for analyzing, automating and optimizing your SAP Business Processes.  Add SAP Build Apps and Graph to the mix and you have a low code option for creating front-end applications.  This particular course will also be available on the SAP Learning site if you prefer to pursue a certificate of course completion there along with a range of complementary courses on SAP Signavio.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/transform-your-business-processes-with-sap-signavio-and-sap-build?url_id=text-former-openSAP-course

 

Finally, we bid OpenSAP a nostalgic farewell but with renewed enthusiasm for continuing our learning journeys with a refreshed format, impetus and clearer path to SAP Certification.  As both a consumer of these courses and trainer myself I welcome any comments regarding your own experiences both with OpenSAP and SAP Learning, what you think they do well and what they might improve upon.  Happy learning!

 

#OpenSAP #SAPLearning #SAPCertification #SAPLearningTransformationStrategy

entry.title: Where can I explore the potential of Taulia, build a comprehensive vision for its implementation?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Taulia for Working Capital Management in SAP S/4HANA learning journey

Overview
Discover how Taulia cashflow orchestration platform revolutionizes your business's working capital strategy through its innovative financial offerings such as Taulia Dynamic Discounting, Taulia Supply Chain Finance, and Taulia Receivables Finance and how your business will benefit from reduced cash conversion cycles, improved supplier relationships, and optimized liquidity management, ensuring a predictable return on investment and immediate financial gains.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, participants will be able to explore the potential of Taulia, build a comprehensive vision for its implementation, and extend its value across various applications.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 

 

We are here to support you. I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Leveraging SAP S/4HANA to Address 2024 Top Finance Priorities
entry.content:
I had the opportunity to speak at the #MasteringSAP event on May 22nd, shedding light on how SAP Customers utilising S/4HANA can navigate and address the leading financial priorities for 2024. 

Off the back of a report by The Hackett Group: 2024 Finance Key Issues – January 2024, The CFO Agenda by Shawn Fitzgerald, Jim O’Connor and Tom Willman. Looking at the top three priorities I provided practical advice based on existing capabilities that organizations may be unaware of and could implement today.  Below is a summary: 

The top 3 finance priorities for 2024 are hardly unexpected, with organisations continuing to focus on managing costs, gaining access to insights to support informed decisions making and securing working capital to execute plans. The key insights that I derived from the report are: 

1. Cost Management and Optimisation

  • Finance increase workload will continue to create productivity and efficiency gaps – in other words we need to find a way to do more with less. 
  • Finance are counting on technology to fill the gaps – currently high levels of dissatisfaction as not necessarily seeing expected benefits. 
  • Wise to explore generative AI as part of a deliberate and sustainable cost reduction approach – expected over next five to seven years result in a 40% reduction in SG&A costs and staff

 

2. Cash Flow Performance, Liquidity and Working Capital 

  • Cost of Working Capital is on the rise, with liquidity coming at a cost premium 
  • Finance must optimize working capital – by focusing on strengthening supplier terms, inventory management and collections management. 

 

3. Data (Timeliness, Completeness, Reliability), Insights and Actionable Analytics 

  • Organisations are not fully deploying data-related technologies 
  • Staff need to be trained and upskilled on using tools 
  • Need greater access to self-service and data discovery tools 
  • Need to implement advanced analytics tools 

 

With S/4HANA you have a technology that is designed to improve productivity and efficiency and drive automation it is important that you leverage the underlying technology and the functional capabilities so you don’t fall into a trap of not seeing the benefits of your investment. Below is a snapshot of the practical advice I shared: 

  • Cost Centre Budget Availability Controls – new in S/4HANA is the ability to activate Budget Availability Controls on the Cost Centre. Once enabled this allows you to fully leverage the Cost Centre Budget Report. 

Fig 1 – Cost Center Budget Report 

 

  • Situation Handling – proactively alerts users to exception events that require attention, proposing resolutions or automating the resolution process driving efficiency. You can see this in action based on a Goods Receipt/Invoice Receipt example: https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_5ssphrci 

 

  • Business AI – Joule is SAPs AI Copilot which is powered by Generative AI and designed to empower users in executing everyday tasks more efficiently. Launching this year, you can see an example here: https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_kc0ahwm4 

 

  • Cash Flow Analyzer – provides a clear cash position insights with visibility of future cash flows supporting the ability to make decisions. 

 

Fig 2 – Example of Cash Flow Analyzer 

 

  • Embedded Analytics – with S/4HANA there are over 250 analytical apps that enable real-time reporting via self-service approach driving insights to action. We looked at the different styles of embedded analytics from Smart Business KPIS, Overview Pages, Analytical List pages, Multi dimensional Reports and Dashboards. 

 

Fig 3 – Example of Multi-dimensional report – Trial Balance 

 

  • Advanced Analytics – On top of the embedded analytics within S/4HANA introducing SAP Analytics Cloud opens the opportunity to: 
  • Use Just Ask – ability to ask questions in natural language and get answers using AI. 
  • Predictive Planning – combining the planning with predictive forecasting to accelerate the planning cycle. 
  • Time series Forecasting – predict future values of a series based on historical data. 
  • Smart Predict – the ability to build trusted and actionable predictions without the need to be a data scientist. 

 

For more information on these Advanced Analytics topics take a look at Augmented Analytics | SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Community. 

 

In conclusion, let’s break the mould and not be one of the companies in the survey that have invested in technology yet did not realise the expected benefits, instead let’s cultivate curiosity and explore the realm of possibilities. While I have imparted a few tips, they merely scratch the surface of what can be achieved. I encourage you to take the following steps: 

 

  • Take time to review the Fiori Application Library for Analytical Apps applicable to your organisation or role. https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/  
  • Explore the integrated Automations & Business AI that are currently available. Artificial Intelligence | SAP Business AI 
  • Identify manual tasks and consider employing SAP Build for automation. 

 

By embracing curiosity and investigation, you’re one step closer to streamlining your finance processes and focusing on driving your business forward.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Solutions for Issuing Electronic Documents to Cloud
entry.content:
(Portuguese Version)

Hi everyone, 

In this blog post, you’ll find advice and solutions to avoid common incidents when issuing an NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, or MDF-e documents to the cloud using the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

Verification of Entitlement for Outbound Invoicing Option for Brazil in the SAP BTP Cockpit

Confirm with your company who the global account administrator is. Only the person who has access to the global account can generate the credentials.

If you need help, refer to User and Member Management from the SAP Business Technology Platform. If the global account administrator or user with access to the global account is no longer with the company, you must create a ticket for the BC-NEO-CIS component to request a new access.

Depending on how your solution was licensed, a second global account may have been generated. If this was not the intention, you need to check with the commercial area to review the contract. 

 Certificate Error When Sending Documents to the Cloud

Generate a certificate authority chain following the procedure mentioned on the Generating a Certificate Authority Chain page. When you upload the certificate for the first time, you don’t need to provide the Company Code and the Plant fields. Additionally, you don’t have to upload the same certificate to every SAP ERP client. The first upload process will be available for the other systems as well. 

Document Remains In Process Status 

If your NF-e document is In Process status or is not returned in a productive system, you can check the following resources to solve the issue:  

  1. Confirm that you have scheduled the jobs mentioned on the Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e page in your system. These jobs can be used to automate various configurations of the service and can be run periodically.  
  2. Check if SEFAZ is online and available. When SEFAZ is offline or unavailable, you can send the document by contingency. Check the Learn about Existing Contingencies in the Electronic Nota Fiscal Solution in ERP blog post (scroll down for the English version) for further information on contingency solutions. 
  3.  Check and review your customizing. On the NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e Specifics page, you can search for the customizing specific for your SAP system. 

Always keep your system updated, check the required SAP Notes 2671909 for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

Note: the NFE_SEND_DOCUMENT_TO_CLOUD job generates numbers for the NF-e (55), CT-e (57) and NFS-e (00) models. If you need to exclude the NF-e and CT-e models from the J_BNFECALLRFC job, you can implement the EXCLUDE_NFES_FROM_BATCH method in the CL_NFE_PRINT BAdI. 

To send and receive MDF-e documents using the outbound invoicing option, you can use the CL_NFE_CLOUD_MDFE_PROCESSOR class in your own solution.

Advice for NFS-e

  • If the RPS (Recibo Provisório de Serviço) field for Outbound is not numbered, follow the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3256626 for troubleshooting information. 
  • If you need to cancel an NFS-e that was not approved, follow the SAP Knowledge Base Article 3207697 for troubleshooting information. 
  • The city of São Paulo does not permit the generation of NFS-e documents in test systems. When the NFS-e document is sent, you will receive an internal message from the outbound invoicing option for Brazil.

Other Resources 

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the SAP Help Portal page SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil. 

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in the SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560. 

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT 
  • LOD-LH-NFE 

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool. 

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “Like”, share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. 

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community to keep up with the latest NF-e news. 

Thank you, and until next time, 
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Soluções para emissão de documentos eletrônicos para a nuvem
entry.content:
(English Version)

Olá! 

Neste blog post, você encontrará recomendações e soluções para evitar incidentes comuns ao emitir documentos NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, ou MDF-e para a nuvem usando o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Verificação de direito para opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil no SAP BTP Cockpit 

Confirme com sua empresa quem é a pessoa que administra a global account. Somente a pessoa que tem acesso à global account pode gerar as credenciais.  

Se precisar de ajuda, consulte a página Gerenciamento de usuários e membros do SAP Business Technology Platform. Se a pessoa que administra a global account ou o usuário com acesso à global account não estiver mais na empresa, você deverá criar um ticket para o componente BC-NEO-CIS para solicitar um novo acesso.  

Dependendo de como sua solução foi licenciada, uma segunda global account pode ter sido gerada. Se essa não foi a intenção, você precisa verificar com a área comercial da sua empresa para revisar o contrato. 

Erro de certificado ao enviar documentos para a nuvem 

Gere uma cadeia de autoridade certificadora seguindo o procedimento mencionado na página Gerar uma cadeia de autoridade certificadora. Quando você carrega o certificado pela primeira vez, não precisa fornecer os campos Company Code e Plant. Além disso, você não precisa carregar o mesmo certificado em todos os clientes SAP ERP. O primeiro processo de upload também estará disponível para os outros sistemas. 

O documento permanece no status Em Processamento 

Se o seu documento NF-e tiver o status Em Processamento ou não for devolvido em um sistema produtivo, você pode verificar os seguintes recursos para resolver o problema:  

  1. Confirme que você escalonou os jobs mencionados na página Escalonar jobs para NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e em seu sistema. Esses jobs podem ser usados para automatizar várias configurações do serviço e podem ser executados periodicamente.  
  2. Verifique se a SEFAZ está online e disponível. Quando a SEFAZ está offline ou indisponível, você pode enviar o documento por contingência. Verifique a postagem Saiba mais sobre as contingências existentes na solução de Nota Fiscal Eletrônica no ERP (role para baixo para a versão em inglês) para obter mais informações sobre soluções de contingência. 
  3. Verifique e revise seu customizing. Na página Específicos da NF-e, CT-e e NFS-e, você pode procurar o customizing específico para seu sistema da SAP. 

Mantenha sempre seu sistema atualizado, verifique a SAP Note 2671909, com as SAP Notes necessárias para SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Preste atenção: o job NFE_SEND_DOCUMENT_TO_CLOUD gera números para os modelos NF-e (55), CT-e (57) e NFS-e (00). Se você precisar excluir os modelos NF-e e CT-e do job J_BNFECALLRFC, pode implementar o método EXCLUDE_NFES_FROM_BATCH na BAdI CL_NFE_PRINT. 

Para enviar e receber documentos MDF-e utilizando a opção de faturamento de saída, você pode utilizar a classe CL_NFE_CLOUD_MDFE_PROCESSOR em sua própria solução.  

Recomendações para NFS-e

  • Se o campo RPS (Recibo Provisório de Serviço) para Saída não estiver numerado, siga o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3256626 para obter informações sobre solução de problemas. 
  • Se você precisar cancelar uma NFS-e que não foi aprovada, siga o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3207697 para obter informações sobre solução de problemas. 
  • A cidade de São Paulo não permite a geração de documentos NFS-e em sistemas de teste. Quando o documento NFS-e for enviado, você receberá uma mensagem interna da opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil. 

Outros recursos 

Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help: 

  • Opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil 
  • Opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil 

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560. 

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT 
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN 
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR 
  • LOD-LH-NFE 

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta. 

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. 

Obrigado e até a próxima, 
Juliana Jarotzky

entry.title: A Natural Choice: SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA
entry.content:
Event-driven architectures are one of the hottest topics in the software industry. Not surprisingly, since EDAs provide substantial benefits like real time updates, optimized business processes or loose coupling - and major mid to long term cost savings.

Events

Events ("significant changes") in these architectures originate from event sources like SAP S/4HANA or SAP SuccessFactors. Once they occur, events are handed over to event brokers that distribute these events to interested event consumers. 

For an introduction to the topic check out our Learning Journey.

SAP offers wonderful event brokers with the outstanding SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh as our go to offering for customers that are serious about event-driven integration, and look for features, scale and full integration into SAP's event-driven ecosystem. 

SAP Event Sources

When I started with EDA seven or eight years ago, the main challenge had been to find suitable events. SAP S/4HANA back then already provided a smaller number of standard events to leverage for your use cases. Nevertheless, those events back then had neither been adjustable nor extensible. So, often you were not able to implement a use case due to a lack of a suitable event.

Over the last years this has changed. Now you can find more than 600 standard events in your SAP S/4HANA, based on RAP. And in the newer SAP S/4HANA versions these events are extensible and adjustable. Plus that you can build your own custom events using RAP in these versions.

So, you would think that all was great on the event source side. Unfortunately, this was not the case. For older backends the challenge still remained. SAP ECC does not provide any standard events at all. Older SAP S/4HANA versions did not allow for the creation of custom events. 

Now, there is SAP AIF.

SAP Application Interface Framework

​SAP Application Interface Framework enables you to develop and monitor interfaces as well as execute error handling in a single framework residing in your SAP back end system. It is based on SAP NetWeaver technology and can therefore work for legacy systems as well. 

​Specifically, AIF allows for both SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA backends: 

  • To use a powerful framework for the implementation of interfaces and the logic behind
  • To monitor interfaces with different interface technologies (e.g. Idocs, ABAP Proxies)

To learn more on AIF check Gautham's great blog.

SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

​SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh is SAP’s main event broker/mesh offering. It is a leading best of breed event broker with almost no scale limits since you can scale either by adding more brokers to the mesh or by increasing the broker size of the individual brokers. It provides a myriad of sophisticated features for state of the art business EDA and is geared towards data & notification events, and 3rd party integration. AEM grows with customer needs from small to extremely large event meshes.

AEM is future-ready for real-time AI and employee initiated EDA. Go for it and most likely there will be no need to ever change your broker technology again.

AIFAEM

So, AEM is SAP’s main event broker/mesh offering.  AIF is being used by thousands of SAP customers as of today. 

​Doesn't it sound like the natural choice to allow older SAP backends to expose custom events based on SAP AIF to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh? This is exactly what the new AIF software component AIFAEM does. 

AIFAEM Features

Using the new AIFAEM software component of AIF, custom events can be created to be exposed via SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh from both SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA systems. This means that AIFAEM offers:

  • Creation of custom events based for SAP backends including SAP S/4HANA and SAP ECC
  • Event creation can be based on for example BOR objects and IDocs
  • Support of CloudEvents format
  • Sophisticated structure mapping
  • Event connectivity to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
  • Monitoring and Error Handling Integration (Transaction /AIF/ERR and SAP Fiori Message Monitoring App)

Supported Releases for AIFAEM

Supported releases for AIFAEM are:

  • SAP NW 7.31 (ECC)
  • SAP NW 7.55 (S/4 HANA 2020) and following

AIFAEM is delivered as ABAP Add-On downloadable by customers

  • AIFAEM731: for SAP NW 7.31
  • AIFAEM800: for SAP NW 7.55, and subsequent releases

Commercials for AIFAEM

This is actually the nicest part: usage of AIFAEM is included with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh.

Additional Information

I understand that there are probably plenty of questions on your side. When do you use AIF in comparison to the standard events, at least in S/4HANA where both are available? How do you get started? Is there any guidance on how to create events?

Answers to these questions will come in the next days and weeks, in additional blogs and additional material.

As a starting point have a look at the note for AIFAEM. 

What I can highly recommend to you as well to get a first impression of the process of creating an event with AIF and exposing it via AEM, is to have look at this blog. Christian walks you through an example of a (classic) BusinessPartner.changed event with a not so classic event type: aif.businesspartner.change. This is not the final approach, it does give you a first impression though.

As said, there is more to come in the next weeks.

Summary

Let me summarize the important points again:

  • AIFAEM is available from today and allows to expose events from SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA
  • It works with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh and the usage is included
  • It is a good choice for older backends and specifically for existing AIF customers
  • It allows for the exposure of custom notification and custom data events
  • The event creation is primarily code template based
  • It includes the great AIF monitoring support

And we highly recommend to stay tuned for more enablement material on AIFAEM to come in the next weeks.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 ‘24 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Q2 2024 has been a whirlwind of activity! We've made substantial advancements to our product with updates such as robust AI capabilities, scalable sandboxes and the introduction of Consumption Credits, all aimed at increased flexibility for your commerce operations.

We took center stage at our annual SAP Sapphire event, proudly showcasing how SAP is leading the way in Business AI. We shined a spotlight on how esteemed customers like Nestlé are leveraging SAP's offerings to provide seamless experiences, coupled with the latest updates on our Intelligent CX portfolio and roadmap. You can discover the many CX highlights by watching the on-demand replays.

As we wind down the first half of the year, we'd like to give you an early heads-up about our 2024 Holiday Season Readiness Program in preparation for the year-end holidays. You must register by August 30, 2024 to participate in this free program.

SAP recently earned the TrustRadius Top Rated and G2 awards for commerce which are a testament to our unwavering commitment to our customers and the trust and value our customers have experienced with SAP Commerce Cloud. We also listen to customer feedback. As an example, the self-service pod restart feature highlighted in this blog was a top requested feature in our SAP Customer Influencer tool.

Let's now dive into the top features that have been rolled out in the past three months for SAP Commerce Cloud. To see our product in action, please view the Q2 2024 Release Highlights webcast to learn more about our latest innovations.

 

Industry-Tailored

Leverage AI to streamline sales of complex products

Let's talk about SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation (SAP IPR), our advanced, cloud-native Business AI tool specially designed for manufacturers and distributors dealing with complex products.

SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation is like the ultimate smart friend we all wish we had. It uses historical data, paired with machine learning, to suggest the most suitable standard or customizable products. But it doesn’t stop there. It can also offer recommendations on areas like default configuration options, price estimates, delivery timeline, total cost of ownership, and even factors like weight - all tailored around the customer’s initially stated needs.

Let’s take a distributor example. The distributor needs to update some of the vehicles in their fleet and desires fuel efficiency and the means to keep products refrigerated in the cab. Now with SAP IPR, instead of configuring technical options, the distributor can specify needs and preferences and receive recommendations of options such as engine types, engine power, cab sizes, cooling systems, and more.

We also provide an intuitive NoCode app builder. This easy-to-use tool empowers business users to create need-based recommendation models that blend human intelligence with machine intelligence. What’s in it for you? Here comes the fun part:

  1. You get consistent B2B product and configuration recommendations that align to your customers’ functional and business needs.
  2. You can drastically cut down on time, effort, and the need for technical know-how to generate a quote or shopping cart.
  3. You offer a genuine self-service e-commerce experience to your customers, reducing the need for sales rep assistance.
  4. You witness faster revenue generation by getting your new hires sale-ready sooner, matching the efficiency and effectiveness of veteran sales reps, and accelerating your expansion into new markets.

SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation

New enhancements to SAP Recommerce for your circular commerce business and sustainability strategy

In the Q1 2024 webcast, we took a deep-dive into SAP Recommerce. Since then, we’ve enhanced the overall ease of use and data visibility for both admin and workbench/business users.

For Admins:

  • You now have richer control over configurable product attributes. You can upload up to 15 customer-specific product attributes into SAP Recommerce alongside the default product data to cater to unique customer needs.
  • Here’s to greater visibility and transparency -- Admins and customers alike can now easily access sustainability data. Admins can import and view sustainability data for each SKU and search for specific products or metrics. Customers can view the imported sustainability data for each item they propose for trade-in via the shopping application.

For Workbench/Business Users:

  • Got a new item for check-in? Users can now manually enter or scan a new barcode to tag that item appropriately. Each item is given a distinct barcode or a digital ID (think: digital passport or serialization) for precise identification.
  • Dig into the details with this new feature—users can view a full list of all traded-in items and can search for specific items using the item’s barcode or unique ID value.
  • Users can effectively capture the default and actual time and cost a particular condition indicator takes to rectify, be it cleaning, repairing or reshaping.
  • Curate your custom workflows -- SAP Recommerce will draft out the steps needed for processing products from "Check In" to "Ready to Restock" based on condition severity, repair requirements, and possible programs (e.g., cleaning only, repair and clean, repair only). Users can select condition indicators that best reflect the evaluation of the product and choose the relevant process flow for the item—say, clean & repair. The subsequent steps get generated automatically and users can process the item until restock.

The overall benefits with these new enhancements are:

  1. Increased product usability across different lines of business and industry solutions
  2. Improved usability and management of trade-in products through intuitive business tools, search, product attribute data display, and flexible process flows
  3. Clear visibility of sustainability data and impact for both business users and customers

Create custom workflows in SAP Recommerce

Telco Accelerator - Empower customers to gain a more thorough understanding of their subscribed service with both fixed and personalized selections

We’ve improved the Telco Accelerator to help you transform faster with this industry-specific capability. Customers will have a more comprehensive understanding of their subscription services, for both fixed and personalized selections. Customers can also easily track modifications to their service contract selections, including pricing updates, providing greater transparency.

Optimize order fulfillment by managing order exceptions and viewing live On Time Performance metrics

Order Management Services now has a workflow to automate the handling of exceptions. If an issue happens during the fulfillment request, the system can re-trigger the fulfillment in some problematic status and apply actions such as re-sourcing or sending a notification to the employee about all these fulfillment requests.

Order Management Services can also now provide live insights for On Time Performance by aggregating all the order data in a single place.  Consume the insights through SAP Analytics Cloud or connect your favorite analytics tool to gain the desired visibility on your order lifecycle data.

Live insights for On Time Performance

Another enhancement is with SAP Order Management Sourcing and Availability, which now supports using contextual updates to provide alternate sourcing results. This includes an updated customer promise for orders in process that cannot be fulfilled as initially sourced. Order sourcing can also respond at any time with multiple service codes for customers to choose the desired fulfillment methods such as standard or express deliveries.

The benefits with these updates are:

  1. Improving the ordering experience for customers, to react swiftly to exceptions occurring during order fulfillment
  2. Providing clear, immediate transparency of order KPIs and trends across all sales channels and fulfillment systems
  3. Understanding omnichannel sales trends early in time to react in other areas, such as promotions, merchandising, and marketing

 

Insightful

Intelligent e-commerce powered by AI

In Q4 2023, we introduced SAP CX AI Toolkit to help you infuse Generative AI for your commerce operations. SAP CX AI Toolkit now has a nifty new feature called "Ask the product". Now, shoppers can ask product-specific questions directly on the storefront! Yes, you read that right. Shoppers can receive answers generated by our very own Gen AI, using product data managed in the backend.

Think of the possibilities—whether it's seeking more information about product attributes and details, or unearthing manuals for intricate B2B products, the world's your oyster. The benefits of “Ask the product” are being able to:

  1. Efficiently locate solutions that are difficult to find within product documentation
  2. Transform your product attributes and descriptions into solutions for customers
  3. Improve your commerce experience with direct and immediate support to your storefront

We’ve also expanded Visual Search beyond the retail industry and into all industries. Visual Search improves product discovery by delivering quick and accurate search results based on AI image detection. So, let the searching spree begin!

 

 

“Ask the product” feature in SAP CX AI Toolkit

Personalize shopping experiences with a virtual AI advisor

New additions to the SAP Store enhance digital experiences by adding more innovation and services to our ever-expanding ecosystem. We are excited to spotlight the FrontNow partnership, giving your shoppers personalized experiences with the use of an AI advisor in customer interactions. This solution helps maximize conversion rates, increase average order value, and boost customer engagement.

FrontNow is a virtual AI advisor, who will joyfully represent your brand in engaging, one-on-one conversations. Shoppers can chat with this friendly advisor in a whopping 85 languages, obtaining tailor-made shopping and service experiences.

The magic behind this? FrontNow's unique ability to gauge customer interactions and delve deep into conversation intentions to self-discover fresh content areas and trend topics.

FrontNow Advisor multilanguage experience

 

Adaptive

Empowering customers with self-service capabilities to scale their infrastructure in non-production environments

At times, you may need to increase the performance of your testing environments. Enter SAP’s game-changing scalable sandboxes.

Our scalable sandboxes allow you to selectively ramp up or dial down specific services within non-production environments by allowing the modification of factors such as the number of instances, CPU, and memory requirements.

Furthermore, we're excited to announce SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits, which is a virtual currency. This virtual currency is sold as Capacity Units.  Using these capacity units, you can self-scale your non-production environments.  The SAP Commerce Cloud's consumption credits will be metered and you have complete usage visibility on SAP4Me (planned towards the end of 2024).

You can purchase the SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits through your Account Executive to cover the costs for any non-production related infrastructure scaling. Please note that Capacity Units depend on yearly consumption and any unused units at the close of the year won't be carried over.

With scalable sandboxes and SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits, customers are:

  1. Empowered with self-service capabilities to define your own infrastructure needs in non-productive environments
  2. Lowering costs by scaling only the infrastructure elements required (database, or VMs, etc.) and by scaling tenants down during quiet periods
  3. Simplifying purchase and consumption of these services through a simple and flexible commercial framework

If you're eager to dive into all the details about scalable sandboxes and Consumption Credits, you can hop over and tune into our recent webcast titled, "Your Key to Agile Scaling! SAP Commerce Cloud, Consumption Credits" webcast.

Capacity Units estimator

 

Safeguard your deployments when releasing significant updates

Currently, when deploying updates, our safeguards include testing a new release on development, QA, and staging environments before it takes center stage in production.

With blue-green deployments, customers can deploy a green environment right alongside a blue one to test it out before opening it to live traffic.

The game-changer here is the Canary deployment. This feature allows customers to transfer a desired percentage of live traffic to the green environment. Think of it like a soft launch to gain more faith in the new release's compatibility with live users. And guess what - this can be specified per endpoint and/or per service for added peace of mind. The benefits of this approach are:

  1. Reducing risk by controlling live customer traffic to new SAP Commerce Cloud deployments
  2. Gaining confidence in significant updates with the ability to smoothly roll-back a deployment with no downtime
  3. Validating your build and deployment quickly and easily

Canary deployment is only available if you subscribed to the previously mentioned SAP Commerce Cloud, consumption credits.

Ensure production environments remain online with Azure SQL Zonal Redundancy

To ensure that any disruption to Azure availability zones does not put production systems at risk, databases are now automatically replicated to different zones within the same region. The redundancy measure helps increase reliability for all customers.

This redundancy protection is our way of boosting reliability for all our customers, making sure your systems are always-on.

Level-up website security with two-factor authentication

Boost your website security with our enhanced two-factor authentication feature! You can now offer your customers an optional email verification for that extra layer of security during the sign-in process, making it significantly harder for attackers to break into accounts, even if credentials somehow fall into the wrong hands.

The best part is, you're always in the driver's seat – you can turn this verification process on or off whenever you want, by simply toggling a switch. The feature also allows you to set the time duration within which a user must input their one-time password, and the number of attempts they get.

We've also strengthened our password storage encryption. This leverages the latest password encoding strategies to further minimize the potential ramifications of any unforeseen data breaches. Even better, we give you the flexibility to customize your own secure strategy by allowing you to choose your preferred hashing mechanism. So, let's level-up your site security today!

Upgraded rules engine for more secure and stronger Commerce Promotions

We have now upgraded the rules engine for our Commerce Promotions with Drools 8. The upgrade enhances the level of security, improves GDPR compliance, and provides extended functionality. It also has backwards compatibility and delivers a more intuitive user experience.

There is an improved Decision Model and Notation (DMN) to ensure better compliance with the latest standards of DMN for robust decision modelling and execution. This update comes with a modern syntax for rules, which is designed to be more developer-friendly. Even without specialist knowledge, developers will find rule writing more intuitive in the integrated development environments (IDE).

We're also excited about Drools’ incorporated AI Integration capability, enabling customers to create advanced decision-making when plugging in machine learning engines. The new capability empowers businesses to carry out intelligent, adaptive, and autonomous business process automation.

Optimizing your Commerce solution with functional and technical assistance service

When it comes to guidance, our SAP experts are here to lend you or your partner a hand with our functional and technical assistance service. You can receive help from SAP CX experts with one-time, functional or technical expertise in a specified area, such as advisory, configuration, or feature activation. This service provides up to 16 hours of consulting support to help you get over unforeseen hurdles quickly.

Save time and gain control on restarting individual pods

You asked, and we listened. In this highly requested feature, administrators can now restart specific pods as required from the Portal without needing help of SAP Support teams. This saves time and gives customers greater control. You can also see a list of restarted pods to view the logs or do some troubleshooting. To avoid any potential disruption to service performance or availability, we recommend waiting for one pod to finish restarting before trying to restart another.
